title: Equinor Looks Overseas to Keep the Barrels Flowing
summary: Norway likes to talk a big game about energy transition, but Equinor is busy reminding everyone that oil and gas are still paying the bills. The Norwegian major plans to sharply increase its international oil and gas production by 2030, pushing overseas output above 900,000 barrels of oil equivalen…

title: China’s LNG Imports Are Poised for a Rebound This Year
summary: China’s LNG imports are set to rebound this year, rising by between 3% and 10% from 2025, driven by lower prices that are expected with the new supply wave, analysts have told Reuters. Purchases from China, the world’s top LNG importer, are set to total between 70.5 million and 75.5 million metric …

title: BP suspends share buyback plan in fresh sign of oil price pressure
summary: BP on Tuesday posted fourth-quarter profit in line with expectations, after crude prices dipped below $60 a barrel for the first time in nearly five years.

title: BP Suspends Buybacks to Strengthen Balance Sheet
summary: BP (NYSE: BP) is suspending share buybacks and retiring the goal to return 30-40% of operating cash flow to shareholders, as the supermajor looks to strengthen its balance sheet amid intense shareholder pressure. BP reported on Tuesday fourth-quarter earnings generally in line with expectations, bu…

title: BP becomes first oil major to suspend share buyback plan
summary: London-listed group says it will focus on strengthening its balance sheet

title: BASF Chief Issues Urgent Warning on EU Emissions Trading Scheme
summary: The head of BASF has warned the European Union needs to update its “obsolete” emissions trading scheme and reconsider the phaseout of free credits to avoid disaster for the chemicals industry.
